steps.title: Identify the uncertainty the discovery revealed
steps.content: The passage concludes: “This means that Ames may not have created the pledge after all.” The discovery of an article published several months before August 1892 directly undermined the established belief about who authored the pledge.

steps.title: Match uncertainty to the answer
steps.content: B identifies what became uncertain: the original author's identity. That earlier article challenges Ames's authorship claim, so we no longer know who truly wrote it. The other choices reference facts the passage never mentions. That matches Choice B — The identity of the person who first wrote the pledge.
